analysis.summary: Prometheus is a systems and service monitoring time-series database written in Go that collects metrics via HTTP pull model, stores them in a custom TSDB, and provides PromQL query language for analysis. It implements a comprehensive monitoring stack with service discovery, alerting, and federation capabilities, designed for cloud-native environments with autonomous single-node architecture.
capabilities: Scrapes metrics from HTTP endpoints with configurable intervals and timeout handling (scrape/scrape.go)
Stores time-series data in a custom TSDB with WAL and compression support (tsdb/db.go, tsdb/head.go)
Executes PromQL queries for time-series analysis and aggregation (promql/engine.go, promql/functions.go)
Provides service discovery for 25+ platforms including Kubernetes, AWS EC2, Consul, DNS (discovery/ directory)
Manages alerting rules evaluation and notification routing to Alertmanager (rules/manager.go, notifier/notifier.go)
Handles remote write/read protocols for external storage integration (storage/remote/write.go, storage/remote/read.go)
Implements label-based relabeling and metric transformation (model/relabel/relabel.go)
Supports federation for hierarchical Prometheus deployments (web/federate.go)
Manages TSDB compaction and retention policies (tsdb/compact.go, tsdb/retention.go)
Processes exemplars for trace correlation with metrics (model/exemplar/exemplar.go)
Handles native histograms and classic histogram conversion (model/histogram/convert.go)
Provides web UI and HTTP API for query and configuration management (web/web.go, web/api/v1/api.go)
Implements configuration hot-reloading without service restart (config/reload.go)
Supports agent mode for metrics forwarding without local storage (tsdb/agent/db.go)
Manages WAL (Write-Ahead Log) for durability and crash recovery (tsdb/wlog/wlog.go)
Implements chunk encoding with multiple compression algorithms (tsdb/chunkenc/chunk.go)
Handles target group management for service discovery (discovery/targetgroup/targetgroup.go)
Provides templating engine for alert annotations and labels (template/template.go)
Implements distributed tracing integration with OpenTelemetry (tracing/tracing.go)
Manages TSDB snapshots and backup/restore operations (tsdb/db.go snapshot methods)
Handles HTTP-based configuration and rule file validation (cmd/promtool/main.go)
Implements concurrent query execution with resource limiting (promql/engine.go)
Provides metrics exposition format parsing for Prometheus and OpenMetrics (model/textparse/)
Manages plugin system for extending functionality (plugins/ directory)
Handles authentication and authorization via various methods (web/authentication.go)
use_cases: DevOps teams use this to monitor infrastructure and application metrics with alerting capabilities
SRE engineers use this for observability, SLI/SLO tracking, and incident response automation
Platform teams use this to provide centralized monitoring infrastructure for microservices architectures
Data engineers use this for time-series data collection and analysis in data pipelines
Cloud-native developers use this for Kubernetes monitoring with auto-discovery of services and pods
analysis.target_audience: DevOps engineers, Site reliability engineers, Platform engineers, System administrators
technical_stack: Protocol Buffers for remote write protocol (prompb/)
Kubernetes client-go for Kubernetes service discovery (go.mod)
Docker and Docker Compose for containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose files)
Prometheus client libraries for metrics exposition (go.mod)
api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/rules - Get alerting and recording rules (web/api/v1/api.go)
integrations: Kubernetes API server for service discovery (discovery/kubernetes/)
AWS EC2 API for instance discovery (discovery/aws/)
Remote storage systems via remote write/read protocols (storage/remote/)

components: TSDB engine for time-series storage and querying
Scrape manager for metrics collection from targets
PromQL query engine for data analysis
Service discovery manager for target discovery
Rule manager for alerting and recording rules
Notifier for alert routing to Alertmanager
Web server providing HTTP API and UI
Remote storage interfaces for external integration
entry_points: cmd/promtool/main.go - Command-line tool for validation and querying
architecture.data_flow: Metrics are scraped from HTTP endpoints by the scrape manager, ingested into TSDB with WAL for durability, compressed and stored in chunks. PromQL queries are executed against the TSDB, with results served via HTTP API. Rules are evaluated periodically, generating alerts sent to Alertmanager or recording new metrics.
architecture.persistence: Custom TSDB with WAL (Write-Ahead Log), chunk-based storage with compression, automatic compaction and retention policies
architecture.concurrency_model: Goroutine-based concurrency with sync/atomic for coordination, concurrent query execution with resource limiting
architecture.communication_patterns: HTTP pull model for metrics scraping, HTTP API for queries, protobuf-based remote write/read for external storage
architecture.plugin_extension_model: Plugin system via Go plugins loaded at startup (plugins/ directory), service discovery plugins for new platforms

primary_entities: Time series with metric name and label dimensions
Metadata for metric types and help text
storage_backends: Custom TSDB with local disk storage
data_model.schema_patterns: Time-series data model with metric names and key-value label pairs, stored in append-only chunks with compression
data_model.migration_strategy: TSDB format versioning with automatic migration on startup

algorithms.problem_type: Time-series monitoring and alerting with multi-dimensional data querying
algorithms.complexity_indicators: Large codebase with 696 Go files, complex TSDB implementation, extensive service discovery integrations
algorithms.evidence: tsdb/db.go implements core TSDB operations, promql/engine.go implements query execution, scrape/scrape.go implements metrics collection

security_model.authentication: HTTP Basic Auth, Bearer tokens, mutual TLS, OAuth2
security_model.authorization: No built-in RBAC - relies on external proxies or authentication layers
security_model.data_protection: TLS for data in transit, no built-in encryption at rest
security_model.security_headers: Configurable CORS settings, standard HTTP security headers
security_model.vulnerability_handling: SECURITY.md file present, dependency scanning via GitHub Actions

dev: Node.js and npm for web UI development

deployment.container_orchestration: Docker Compose support, Kubernetes manifests available
deployment.environment_config: YAML configuration files, environment variables, command-line flags
deployment.scaling_model: Horizontal scaling via federation, vertical scaling for single instances

project_health.recent_activity: Active development with merge of revive linting improvements
project_health.community_signals: Complete community health files: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, MAINTAINERS.md

information_gaps: Exact API endpoint implementations require deeper code reading

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: The system continuously collects metrics from distributed endpoints via pull model, aggregates time-series data with configurable intervals, and provides query-based access to historical aggregated data. It implements comprehensive data aggregation with retention policies and compaction strategies.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Uses Prometheus exposition format and OpenMetrics with defined metric types, but accepts flexible label dimensions. Protocol Buffers for remote write/read, YAML configuration, and PromQL query language indicate structured but extensible schemas.
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: TSDB chunk compression algorithms, time-based data compaction, WAL for durability, label-based series indexing, and PromQL query engine with temporal aggregation functions. Core focus on temporal data storage and retrieval optimization.
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Goroutine-based concurrency with sync/atomic coordination, pull-based collection model tolerates temporary failures, WAL provides durability without strict ACID requirements, federation allows distributed deployment with eventual consistency.
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Continuously aggregates timestamped measurements from distributed sources into a queryable time-series repository with retention management and multi-dimensional indexing for temporal pattern analysis.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: Periodic pull collection → WAL persistence → chunk compression → indexed storage → query engine → API response, with parallel rule evaluation generating derived metrics and alerts
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: The time-series database engine, query language, and collection orchestration are highly generic patterns applicable to any domain requiring temporal data aggregation. The plugin-based service discovery and HTTP pull model are domain-agnostic. Only the metrics exposition format and some monitoring-specific terminology are domain-locked. The core TSDB, PromQL engine, and federation capabilities could be repurposed for financial data, IoT sensors, or scientific measurements.

evidence_claims.claim: Implements custom time-series database with WAL and compression
evidence_claims.evidence: tsdb/db.go, tsdb/head.go, tsdb/wlog/wlog.go, tsdb/chunkenc/chunk.go files implement TSDB storage engine

evidence_claims.claim: Uses pull-based collection model with configurable intervals
evidence_claims.evidence: scrape/scrape.go implements HTTP endpoint scraping with timeout handling

evidence_claims.claim: Provides domain-specific query language for temporal analysis
evidence_claims.evidence: promql/engine.go, promql/functions.go implement PromQL query engine with aggregation functions
discovery/ directory contains platform-specific discovery implementations including Kubernetes, AWS EC2, Consul, DNS
plugins/ directory and service discovery plugin system for extending functionality

cross_industry_applications: Financial Services - High-frequency trading data aggregation and market surveillance with temporal queries
IoT/Manufacturing - Industrial sensor data collection from distributed devices with time-series analysis
Scientific Research - Laboratory instrument data aggregation with temporal pattern analysis and alerting
Telecommunications - Network performance metrics collection from distributed infrastructure components
Energy/Utilities - Smart grid data aggregation from meters and sensors with usage pattern analysis
Healthcare - Medical device data aggregation with patient monitoring and alert generation
Retail/E-commerce - Customer behavior metrics aggregation with time-series trend analysis
Supply Chain - Logistics tracking data aggregation with temporal visibility across distribution networks

complexity_factors: Custom TSDB implementation requires deep understanding
friction_report.migration_path: Extract TSDB engine and query components, replace metrics exposition format with domain-specific data model, adapt service discovery to domain-specific sources, modify query language for domain terminology

output.format: JSON, Protocol Buffers, Prometheus exposition format
output.delivery: sync HTTP responses, streaming for federation

authentication.method: HTTP Basic Auth, Bearer tokens, mutual TLS, OAuth2

comparable_systems: Apache Kafka - Event stream aggregation with retention and temporal querying across distributed topics
ElasticSearch - Document aggregation with time-based indices and query DSL for temporal analysis
InfluxDB - Purpose-built time-series database with continuous queries and retention policies
Apache Druid - Real-time analytics database with time-partitioned data and aggregation queries
Grafana Loki - Log aggregation system with label-based indexing and temporal query capabilities
information_gaps: Specific TSDB storage format and compression algorithm details
Complete PromQL function implementation and query optimization strategies
Detailed federation protocol and hierarchical deployment patterns
Remote storage protocol specifications and adapter implementations
